experiment: real-corpus defensive scan + AI triage (false-positive suppression on real code)

findings.rationale: Untrusted HTTP request parameter is passed directly to eval(), allowing arbitrary code execution.
Untrusted user input from the HTTP request is directly concatenated into an OS command, enabling arbitrary command injection.
pickle.loads is called directly on untrusted HTTP request data, enabling arbitrary code execution via malicious payloads.
This intentionally mimics the standard Python interpreter's PYTHONSTARTUP behavior, executing a user-controlled local startup script to initialize the shell context, which is by-design and not driven
This is Flask's by-design from_pyfile mechanism that intentionally loads trusted local Python config files as modules, a documented and spec-mandated framework feature.
This is a by-design use of exec within Jinja2's debug machinery to regenerate a traceback with an internally-constructed code object, not execution of attacker-controlled input.
This is a by-design use of exec within the Jinja2 template engine to execute compiled template code.
MD5 is used here to compute the SSH public key fingerprint, which is a spec-mandated identification format and not a cryptographic security control.

note: Real third-party source (never executed); GLM-5.2 contextual triage of real semgrep hits. Single non-deterministic run. Demonstrates FP suppression: raw SAST on real code is mostly legitimate/by-design uses.
